page: Amazon Web Services m8id.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 64 vCPUs, corresponding to 32 physical cores with hyperthreading, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The instance is equipped with 256 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance incorporates 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, offering high-speed local data access. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. No dedicated GPU hardware is included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8id.16xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, achieving a Passmark CPU score of 82,895.65 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 21,132.0, both placing in the top tier. Memory performance is robust, with cached small-block read bandwidth reaching 5,110.42 GB/sec and uncached large-block read bandwidth at 196.04 GB/sec. The instance also shows high efficiency in data processing, recording a static web serving throughput of 105.65 GB/sec and a Redis SET performance of 112.74 million operations per second. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the combination of high-density DDR5 memory, local NVMe storage, and dedicated Intel Xeon compute resources provides a balanced resource profile for workloads requiring high throughput without the premium of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances.

The m8id.16xlarge is optimized for demanding general-purpose workloads. It is highly suitable for relational and non-relational databases, static web serving at scale, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 256 GB of DDR5 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance delivers top-tier benchmark performance, including a Passmark CPU score of 82,895.65 and a static web serving throughput of 105.65 GB/sec. It also exhibits strong memory bandwidth, reaching 196.04 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Lacking a dedicated GPU, it relies on Intel AMX for acceleration. This resource configuration provides a cost-effective balance of compute, high-speed local storage, and memory. The m8id.16xlarge is well-suited for database operations, large-scale web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier CPU and web serving benchmark performance.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 platform combining 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, high-speed DDR5 memory, and 3.8 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 64 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
256 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier CPU performance with a Passmark score of 82,895.65
Ideal for databases, static web serving, and software compilation
